blocks.content: She lay in the dark and looked at the ceiling, careful not to follow the feeling all the way to where it was going.
What she kept coming back to was the water tower.
Not the kiss — that was Kit's to give. But the tower. The specific place. She had stood up there in the summer they were twelve and something had almost happened.
He'd taken someone else there. He either knew what he was doing or he didn't.
In the morning she got up and dressed and ate breakfast and her mom watched her from the kitchen doorway.
Daisy picked up her bag and went to school.
